summary: Petition for formal adjudication of intestacy and appointment of a personal representative (or adjudication only).
applies: Title 18-C (Maine Uniform Probate Code), effective 2019-09-01 — see transition_18a.

governing.title: Formal testacy proceedings; nature; when commenced
governing.why: Formal testacy proceeding — this petition asks the JUDGE (not the register) to adjudicate intestacy, after notice and hearing.

governing.title: Formal testacy or appointment proceedings; petition; contents
governing.why: Required contents of a formal testacy/appointment petition.

governing.title: Formal testacy proceeding; notice of hearing on petition
governing.why: Notice of hearing to interested persons; the formal proceeding is litigated on notice.

governing.title: Formal testacy proceedings; order; foreign will
governing.why: After hearing, the court enters an order determining heirs and intestacy.

governing.title: Formal proceedings concerning appointment of personal representative
governing.why: Formal proceedings concerning appointment of a PR.

governing.title: Priority among persons seeking appointment as personal representative
governing.why: Priority among persons seeking appointment as PR.

governing.title: Probate, testacy and appointment proceedings; ultimate time limit
governing.why: Three-year ultimate time limit, subject to its exceptions.

considerations.title: Formal testacy proceedings; nature; when commenced
considerations.note: Formal adjudication and formal appointment may be sought together or adjudication alone (AND/OR checkboxes).

considerations.title: Formal testacy or appointment proceedings; petition; contents
considerations.note: Petitioner must state an interest giving standing to petition.

considerations.title: Probate, testacy and appointment proceedings; ultimate time limit
considerations.note: Death date starts the 3-year clock.

considerations.title: Time of taking effect; provisions for transition
considerations.note: Death date selects 18-C vs former 18-A substantive law.

considerations.title: Probate, testacy and appointment proceedings; ultimate time limit
considerations.note: After 3 years a formal testacy proceeding is limited to the §3-108 exceptions (e.g. determination of heirs).

considerations.title: Share of heirs other than surviving spouse
considerations.note: Heirs are determined by the intestacy statute.

considerations.title: Formal testacy or appointment proceedings; petition; contents
considerations.note: The petition must list heirs so the §3-409 order can adjudicate them.

considerations.title: Share of spouse
considerations.note: Only a registered domestic partner takes a spouse-equivalent intestate share.

considerations.title: Definitions
considerations.note: Statutory definition of 'domestic partner'.

considerations.title: Venue for first and subsequent estate proceedings; location of property
considerations.note: Maine-situs real property supports venue.

considerations.title: Formal testacy or appointment proceedings; petition; contents
considerations.note: Petitioner must either identify an unrevoked testamentary instrument (and explain why it is not being probated) or state that diligent search found none.

considerations.title: Priority among persons seeking appointment as personal representative
considerations.note: Relationship drives statutory priority to serve as PR.

considerations.title: Priority among persons seeking appointment as personal representative
considerations.note: Persons of prior or equal right must be addressed (renunciation, nomination, or notice).

considerations.title: Formal proceedings concerning appointment of personal representative
considerations.note: Formal appointment resolves competing claims of priority.

considerations.title: Formal proceedings concerning appointment of personal representative
considerations.note: Questions of priority or qualification are resolved by the court in the formal proceeding; if none, state 'None.'

considerations.title: Bond not required without court order; exceptions
considerations.note: Bond requirements for a PR; the court may be asked to decide.

considerations.title: Demand for bond by interested person
considerations.note: An interested person may demand bond.

considerations.title: Notice to creditors
considerations.note: Optional published notice to creditors shortens the claims period.

considerations.title: Supervised administration; nature of proceeding
considerations.note: Supervised administration is a single in rem proceeding under continuing court authority.

considerations.title: Priority among persons seeking appointment as personal representative
considerations.note: A person with priority may renounce the right to appointment or concur in the appointment sought.

transition_18a: Title 18-C applies to estates of decedents who die on or after 2019-09-01, and to any proceeding pending on or commenced after that date. For a decedent who died before 2019-09-01, former Title 18-A supplies the substantive succession rules even though the proceeding itself runs under 18-C. See _index/18a-key-diffs.json (governing rule: 18-C §8-301).

cross_refs.title: Maine estate tax: tax due date; filing of return and payment

caselaw.name: In re Estate of Kruzynski
caselaw.topic: Three-year ultimate time limit to commence probate/appointment
caselaw.holding: No informal or formal probate or appointment proceeding may be commenced more than three years after the decedent's death (the ultimate time limit). (Former 18-A §3-108; carried forward verbatim in substance to 18-C §3-108.)
